task_completion.description: Zotero application is open and the tag selector pane is visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Zotero main window is open, and the tag selector pane (usually located in the bottom-left corner) is visible, displaying various tags.

task_completion.description: Agent interacts with the tag selector to delete the specified obsolete tags.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent right-clicking on tags in the tag selector pane and selecting 'Delete Tag...' or a similar deletion option from the context menu.

task_completion.description: The 6 specified obsolete tags are successfully deleted and no longer visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the final screenshots, the tags 'imported', 'to-read-maybe', 'uncategorized', 'DUPLICATE', '_temp', and 'needs-review' are completely absent from the Zotero tag selector pane.

task_completion.description: The 5 specified good tags are preserved and remain visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the final screenshots, the tags 'deep-learning', 'computer-science', 'information-theory', 'physics', and 'NLP' are still clearly visible in the Zotero tag selector pane.

task_completion.description: No library items (papers) were deleted during the process.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The central pane of the Zotero window continues to display the list of library items (papers) in the final screenshots, confirming they were not removed.

integrity.description: Agent used the Zotero GUI to delete tags.
integrity.visual_evidence: Tag deletions should be performed via the Zotero graphical interface (e.g., right-click context menus), not via terminal commands or scripts.

integrity.description: Agent did not use terminal commands or SQLite scripts to modify the database directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows or code editors are shown executing `sqlite3` commands or scripts targeting the `zotero.sqlite` file.

integrity.description: The interaction occurred within the genuine Zotero application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface matches the standard Zotero layout (collections pane on the left, items list in the center, item details on the right, tag selector bottom-left).

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires deleting exactly 6 junk tags: 'imported', 'to-read-maybe', 'uncategorized', 'DUPLICATE', '_temp', and 'needs-review'. Exactly 5 good tags must be preserved: 'deep-learning', 'computer-science', 'information-theory', 'physics', and 'NLP'. The tags are located in the Zotero tag selector pane (usually bottom left). The central pane must continue to show the library items (papers) to confirm they were not deleted.
